rootCauses.title: Dual-Identity Architecture Problem
rootCauses.description: Two independent CLI systems (Build With Nexus docker-based and Deep Agents python-based) were merged at entry point level without unifying subsystems. Results in parallel implementations of config, keys, backend lifecycle, UI, and model constants that are unaware of each other.

rootCauses.title: No Shared Model Constants Registry
rootCauses.description: Model strings hardcoded in 3+ locations with different naming conventions. bin.ts uses 'claude-sonnet-4-20250514', agent-templates.ts uses 'claude-sonnet-4-5'. No central MODEL_CONSTANTS object.

rootCauses.title: No Backend Lifecycle Management in Interactive Mode
rootCauses.description: Interactive mode assumes backend is running but provides no mechanism to start it. Health check fails → exit. Does not use startNexus() from docker.ts like System A does.

findings.title: Dual-CLI merger creates header duplication and banner conflicts
findings.description: Two independent header-rendering systems: printAppHeader() in interactive.ts, showBanner() in ui/banner.ts, showShellBanner() in commands/shell.ts. When user enters sub-REPL or switches modes, both systems' banners can fire simultaneously creating duplicate/conflicting headers.
findings.impact: User sees duplicated headers (DEEP AGENTS + Nexus) with bad ASCII alignment. Confusing and unprofessional appearance.
findings.remediation: Unify entry point: (1) Merge bin.ts and cli.ts into single Commander program. (2) Remove dual entry points. (3) Create single banner function called once at startup. (4) Add banner-shown state to prevent re-rendering.

findings.title: Hardcoded model strings instead of shared constants
findings.description: Model strings hardcoded in bin.ts:41 ('claude-sonnet-4-20250514'), deep-agents-bin.ts:117 (same), and agent-templates.ts:15-85 (uses 'claude-sonnet-4-5'). No shared MODEL_CONSTANTS or MODEL_REGISTRY. Three different model naming conventions across codebase.
findings.impact: Model updates require finding and changing hardcoded strings in 3+ locations. Risk of inconsistent models across CLI and backend. Difficult to maintain and update model versions.
findings.remediation: Create src/core/models.ts exporting DEFAULT_MODEL, OPUS_MODEL, SONNET_MODEL, HAIKU_MODEL constants. Replace all 9 hardcoded strings. Map agent-templates to use shared constants.

findings.title: Backend not auto-started when buildwithnexus interactive mode launches
findings.description: Interactive mode performs health check at localhost:4200. If fails, exits with error 'Start it with: buildwithnexus server' but no 'server' command exists. System A (Docker) auto-starts via startNexus(). System B (interactive) has no equivalent.
findings.impact: User must manually start backend before using buildwithnexus interactive mode, violating UX expectation that tool is self-contained. Error message references non-existent command.
findings.remediation: (1) Add buildwithnexus server command. (2) In interactive mode, if health check fails, attempt to start backend via startNexus() or spawn Python process. (3) Use health check with retries before giving up.

findings.title: Conflicting key management strategies between System A and System B
findings.description: System A stores keys in ~/.buildwithnexus/.env.keys (HMAC-sealed, persistent). System B stores in ~/.env.local (plain dotenv, always re-prompted). Two separate key paths never synchronized. start.ts:31 references nonexistent config.anthropicKey causing runtime error.
findings.impact: Keys stored in two locations. User configured via one path may have different keys via another path. start command breaks at runtime due to missing config properties.
findings.remediation: Unify to single key storage. Choose HMAC-sealed approach (System A) for security. Migrate System B to use same store. Fix start.ts to use loadKeys(). Add 'use cached or re-enter' prompt instead of always overwriting.

findings.title: Mode switching mechanism (SHIFT+SPACE) documented but not implemented
findings.description: User documentation mentions SHIFT+SPACE for mode switching but this keybinding does not exist anywhere in codebase. Actual mode switching uses text prompts: type 'plan'/'build'/'brainstorm' or use numbered options. Readline interface does not expose raw keypress events.
findings.location: src/ui/repl.ts:54-62 (readline-based), src/cli/tui.ts:190 ([s] switch mode hint), src/cli/interactive.ts:81-111 (text-based selectMode)
findings.impact: Users cannot use documented SHIFT+SPACE shortcut. Mode switching is text-based, less discoverable. No raw keypress handling available for sleek UX.
findings.remediation: Option A: Implement raw keypress handling with process.stdin.setRawMode(true) and custom keypress event listener. Option B: Document actual mechanism ([s] for switch) and drop SHIFT+SPACE expectation. Recommend Option B for TTY compatibility.

findings.title: API keys transmitted in cleartext HTTP request bodies
findings.description: Anthropic, OpenAI, Google API keys are serialized into JSON request bodies and sent via HTTP (not HTTPS) to backend at localhost:4200. If user changes BACKEND_URL to remote host, keys are transmitted in plaintext. Keys sent on every request instead of server-side configuration.
findings.impact: If backend is on remote/public host, API keys exposed in transit. OWASP A02:2021 Cryptographic Failures. Keys unnecessarily transmitted per-request.
findings.remediation: (1) Validate BACKEND_URL uses HTTPS when non-localhost. (2) Prefer server-side key configuration. (3) If per-request required, warn user when URL is non-local and non-HTTPS. (4) Consider only accepting localhost for key transmission.

findings.title: TypeScript type errors in tui.ts - chalk.bold() not available on color function type
findings.description: tui.ts:184, :215, :231 call .bold() on color fields typed as (s: string) => string. Chalk-specific chain methods (.bold, .underline, etc.) require Chalk type, not just function signature. Code compiles only due to skipLibCheck: true.
findings.impact: Type safety violation. IDE does not catch error. Maintenance hazard if Chalk types tighten in future versions. Project has 15 total tsc errors across 7 files.
findings.remediation: Type color fields as chalk.ChalkInstance or chalk.Chalk instead of (s: string) => string. Example: const modeConfig: Record<Mode, { icon: string; color: chalk.Chalk }> = { ... }

findings.title: Duplicated SSE stream parsing logic in 4 locations
findings.description: SSE stream reading pattern (buffer + decode + split + parse 'data: ' prefix + JSON.parse) copy-pasted in: interactive.ts:228-258 (plan), :366-406 (build), :469-516 (brainstorm), run-command.ts:69-104. Each handles errors slightly differently.
findings.impact: Maintenance hazard. A bug fix (e.g., multi-byte UTF-8 handling) must be applied 4 times. Risk of inconsistent fixes across copies.
findings.remediation: Extract shared async generator: async function* parseSSEStream(reader: ReadableStreamDefaultReader) yields {type, data}. Import in all 4 locations.

findings.title: Module-level mutable state in init-command.ts causes cross-invocation bugs
findings.description: init-command.ts:63-65 defines module-level inputLines, lineIndex, isSetup variables. Once isSetup=true, subsequent calls to deepAgentsInitCommand() reuse stale inputLines from previous run. In TTY mode works by accident. Fragile design.
findings.impact: If interactive mode calls deepAgentsInitCommand() multiple times in single process, second call reuses stale data. Bug only manifests with piped input, but design is fundamentally unsound.
findings.remediation: Encapsulate state in class instantiated per invocation, or reset state at start of deepAgentsInitCommand(). Avoid module-level mutable state.

findings.title: Stale fallback version string in bin.ts
findings.description: bin.ts:20 hardcodes fallback version '0.5.17' while package.json is at 0.7.1. If __BUILDWITHNEXUS_VERSION__ not injected, CLI reports wrong version. Missing ambient type declaration causes tsc error on lines 18-19.
findings.impact: If build step skipped or __BUILDWITHNEXUS_VERSION__ not injected, CLI reports outdated version (0.5.17 vs 0.7.1). Users confused about which version they're running.
findings.remediation: (1) Add declare const __BUILDWITHNEXUS_VERSION__: string in .d.ts file. (2) Read version from package.json at runtime or sync fallback via build script. (3) Keep fallback version in sync.

findings.title: Silent swallowing of JSON parse errors in SSE streams
findings.description: interactive.ts:255, :403, :512; run-command.ts:99 use empty catch {} blocks that silently discard parse errors. If backend sends malformed JSON, user sees no feedback. Stream appears to hang indefinitely.
findings.impact: Corrupted stream appears to hang silently. No visibility into why stream stopped. Difficult to debug.
findings.remediation: Log parse errors in debug mode: catch (e) { if (process.env.LOG_LEVEL === 'debug') console.error('SSE parse error:', e); } Preserves silent behavior for production while aiding debugging.

findings.title: readline.Interface created per question instead of reused
findings.description: init-command.ts:105-113 (askQuestion in TTY mode) creates and closes new readline.Interface for each prompt. This causes flickering, inconsistent cursor behavior, wasteful resource churn. Called 5+ times during init.
findings.impact: Poor UX: flickering terminal, cursor inconsistency. Resource waste: creating/destroying readline interface 5+ times. Could slow down initial setup on slow systems.
findings.remediation: Create single readline.Interface at start of deepAgentsInitCommand(), pass it through all askQuestion calls, close once at end.

findings.title: No input validation on backend URL before writing to .env.local
findings.description: init-command.ts:35 accepts user-provided backendUrl without validation and writes directly to .env.local. Malformed URL (with newlines, shell metacharacters) could corrupt file.
findings.impact: Malformed URL in .env.local breaks subsequent runs. User must manually edit file to recover.
findings.remediation: Validate with try { new URL(backendUrl); } catch { reject } before writing.

findings.title: No timeout on backend HTTP requests
findings.description: All fetch() calls to backend have no timeout. Health checks at interactive.ts:32, :208, :343, :446; run-command.ts:18, :34 hang indefinitely if backend doesn't respond.
findings.impact: If backend hangs, CLI hangs indefinitely with no user recourse. Poor UX for timeout scenarios.
findings.remediation: Use AbortSignal.timeout(10000) for health checks, longer timeout (120s) for task execution. Node 18+ built-in.

findings.title: Unused rl parameter threaded through all mode loop functions
findings.description: interactive.ts:117 (runModeLoop signature), :193, :331, :430 all take rl: readline.Interface parameter but never use it. All input via ask callback.
findings.impact: Dead parameter causes confusion when reading code. Reduces clarity about how user input is handled.
findings.remediation: Remove rl parameter from all function signatures.

findings.title: Interactive mode always forces API key setup on every launch
findings.description: interactive.ts:10-13 unconditionally calls deepAgentsInitCommand() which prompts for 3 keys + backend URL + port. Users who already configured keys must press Enter 5 times every launch. Comment says 'Always ask for API keys on startup' but poor UX.
findings.impact: Friction on every launch. User frustration. Poor UX compared to systems that remember configuration.
findings.remediation: Check hasAnyKey() first. Only init if no keys: if (!hasAnyKey()) { await deepAgentsInitCommand(); } Keep da-init command for reconfiguration.

findings.title: Box-drawing functions don't account for ANSI escape code widths
findings.description: tui.ts:263, :280 (makeRoundedBox, makeDoubleBox) use .padEnd(innerWidth) on strings containing ANSI color codes from chalk. Escape sequences add invisible characters, throwing off width calculations. Causes misaligned box borders.
findings.impact: Box borders misaligned in terminal. Unprofessional appearance. Reduced readability of formatted output.
findings.remediation: Use string-width library or strip ANSI codes before padding, apply coloring after. Compute visible width vs. byte width.

findings.title: No visible feedback when API key validation fails
findings.description: init-command.ts:30-33 checks at least one key provided but error message 'At least one API key must be provided' is generic. Doesn't indicate which keys were tried, why validation failed, or what user should do next.
findings.impact: User doesn't know which keys are required or why setup failed. May retry with same invalid input.
findings.remediation: Provide detailed error: 'Please provide at least one of: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, OPENAI_API_KEY, GOOGLE_API_KEY. You entered: Anthropic [empty], OpenAI [empty], Google [empty].'

findings.title: Empty string API keys treated as undefined without user warning
findings.description: config.ts:18-20 uses process.env.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY || undefined which converts empty string to undefined. Whitespace-only keys also treated as absent. User not warned that empty value equals no key.
findings.impact: User sets ANTHROPIC_API_KEY='' thinking it's configured, but it's silently treated as absent. Confusing when debugging missing keys.
findings.remediation: Use .trim() || undefined to handle whitespace. Log warning if env var is set but empty: 'WARNING: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is set but empty'

findings.title: Zero test coverage across entire codebase
findings.description: No .test.ts or .spec.ts files exist. Pure functions in intent-classifier.ts, config.ts, and SSE parsing logic are straightforward to unit test but have no coverage.
findings.impact: No regression detection. Refactoring risky. Intent classifier could break from slight keyword changes without detection.
findings.remediation: Add unit tests for: classifyIntent() (5 tests), loadApiKeys()/hasAnyKey() (8 tests), SSE parsing (once extracted, 6 tests), and happy/sad paths for interactive mode (10 tests). Target ~40 tests total.
